etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Aramaic דּוּכָּנָאbor.
Arabic دُكَّان
From Aramaic דּוּכָּנָא / ܕܘܼܟܵܢܵܐ (dukkānā, “dais”), from Akkadian 𒆠𒍇 (/⁠takkannu, dakkannu, dukkannu⁠/, “dais, platform, bench; niche, bedroom, private chambers or quarters”), ultimately from Sumerian 𒆠𒍇 (/⁠daggan⁠/, “chamber, doorway or its frame, niche in a wall”). See also Hebrew דּוּכָן (dukhán).
